Re: Abuse of Campaign Contributor List
Dear Mr. Larson:
Attached please find a letter myself and two hundred other
contributors of U.S. Conqressman, Dana Rohrbacher, received. An
organization entitled the so-caI1ed trNational Carnpaign for Freedom
of Expressiontr directly stated that they were expressly going to
major campaign donors of U. rS. Congressman Dana Rohrbacher to
object to a particular political 
issue.
It seems to me that the federal law expressly prohibits the
use of this campaign list for this type of solicitation.
It is clear that the co-cal-l-eC rr!'Iaticnal CanpaiEn for Fr-eeciorn
of Expressionrr is not hiding the fact that they are using a
political 
donorrs list which is reguired under the United States
Code, to list all campaign donors for their own purposes, including
potential fund raising.
Please investigate the matter to see which 1aws, if dny, were
violated. 
We appreciate hearing from you in the very near future.
Sinc

enclosure
Sincerely yours,

Michael J. Schroeder

Coalition for Freedom of Expression
Long Beach / Orange County Chapter
June 24, 7990
Shawn Steel
610 South Harvard Boulevard
Suite 200
I,os Angeles, CA 90005
Dear Mr. Steel,
On behalf of the long Beach/Orange County Chapter of the
Coal-ition for Freedom of Expressi-on, we ire writing with troubling
news about the very damaging attack Representative Dana
Rohrabacher (R-Iomita) is teading against the arts and artistic
freedom i-n America.
Our Chapter of the National Campaign for Freedom of Expression is
made up of concerned citizens, community leaders, educators,
artists and arts professionals who live within Rep. Rohrabacher,s
Congressional District. 
While the entire nation stands to l_ose if
Rohrabacher's anti-freedom and anti-art initiatives are enacted,
we feel- that it is our special responsibility to lead the fight
for freedom and for the arts within the communities he was elected
to represent.
We know that you have been a significant campaj-gn contributor to
Rep. Rohrabacher, and respect your right to support the candidate
of your choice. But, it has come to our attention that many of
Congressman Rohrabacher's supporters may not be fully aware of his
dangerous position on the issue of artiltic 
freedorn lnd
censorship,- nor the fatal irnplications of legislation he sponsors
regarding the arts and t.he substance and spiiit of the Firit
-Amendment.
At a time when democracy and freedom are breaking out al1 over the
world., it is sadly ironic that in "the land of tfre freer', Rep.
Rohrabacher and others are pushing the u.s. congress to'the Lrink
of adopting chilling restrictions on artistic fieedom. In many
eastern-bloc countries, it was the artists who led the democratic
revorutions while in Ameri-ca, senator Jesse Helms and congressman
Rohrabacher lead the fight against artistic freedom. Thei are
demanding that artists ind aits organizations submit to--pii".
restraint of free speech--forcing thern to sign ;r"viri" -o"tir"" n"t
to make or present work that might offend sofi.one-'-in -lrJ.i t"
receive Federal funding.
Helms and Rohrabacher clairn that the issue is sponsorship, not
censorship. and this may seem persuasive on casual consideration.


But sponsorship is being used here to enforce censorship because
it is predicated upon the restraint of free speech. As Chief
Justice William Rehnqui6t wrote for the Supreme Court in a
unanimous 1983 opinion, neither bv penaltv-nor subsidy may the
government "aim at the suppression of dangerous ideas.,'
Not only is artistic freedom in dire jeopardy, but worse stil1,
Rohrabacher and some of his colleagues are pressing for outright
elimination of the National Endowment for the Arts. The NEA is
one of the great success stories in America. It has conpletely
transformed public accessibility to the arts while generatj-ng
controversy in only a minute handful of the over 801000 projects
it has funded in 25 years. And it costs each citizen only ibout
68 cents per year, the lowest arts contribution by any
industrialized democracy, and a very minor part of the national
budget.
That any serious consideration is being given to eliminating an
agency with such a track record of exceLlence and responsibility
attests to the distorted case being manufactured by its
detractors. That Representative Rohrabacher and his allies have
inflated this case to the point where it preoccupies Congress,
while such expensive and pressing concerns such as the budget
deficit, crime, drugs, homeless Americans, and the environment cry
out for Congressional attention, makes us wonder where our
Representative's priorities lie.
The founders of our nation were guided by a visionary commitrnent
to diversity. 
The suppression of dJ-versity, the censorship of
artistic expression, is one of the hallmarks of an undemocratic
society. It is something that Americans have been proud to
denounce in other governments. All free societies were outraged
when Iran condemned Salman Rushdie for his novel The Satanic
verses. All f ree societies cheered when the greaE-EIEEh--
playwright Vaclav Ilavel, once censored by his government, becarne
president of his newly democratized country.
As members of the greatest free society in the world, and the
model for the new democracies of the world, we must lead the fight
against censorship and the suppression of free expression. The-
National Endowment for the Arli must be encouragel to support a
wide range of artists and arts spaces. It must not be ciippled by
restrictions or censorship, or suffer elimination
P1ease contact Rep. Rohrabacher and ask hirn to reconsider his
dangerously misguided position.
:?L tl? Memhership .of ttle Long Beach/Orange County Chapter of the
coalrt.ron tor Freedom of Expression, I am
Sincerely youls,

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the use of a campaign contributor list by the Coalition for Freedom of Expression to solicit major donors of U.S. Congressman Dana Rohrabacher regarding a political issue violates federal or California law.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming complaint/request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document includes the complaint letter from attorney Schroeder and two copies of the Coalition's solicitation letter to campaign contributors. Confidence reduced due to unclear document boundaries and lack of formal FPPC structure.

embedding.summary: Michael J. Schroeder writes to FPPC Chairman John Larson on behalf of 200+ contributors to Congressman Dana Rohrabacher, alleging that the Coalition for Freedom of Expression improperly used Rohrabacher's campaign donor list to solicit these contributors regarding the Representative's position on artistic freedom and the National Endowment for the Arts, and requesting investigation of potential federal law violations.
